symptoms.symptom_name: Absolute refusal of all food and fluid for 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe dehydration with acute kidney injury
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: 48h without fluid is a direct and reversible cause of severe hypovolaemia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe adult failure to thrive / terminal frailty
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absolute anorexia is a feature of end-stage decline, but non-specific.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Delirium from occult infection / sepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New fluctuating confusion in a frail elder is delirium until proven otherwise and signals an acute cause.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe dehydration with acute kidney injury
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypovolaemia and uraemia cause confusion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe adult failure to thrive / terminal frailty
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Terminal decline can include confusion, but acute change must first be worked up as delirium.

symptoms.symptom_name: New inability to stand / bear weight — acute functional collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute-on-chronic decompensation of a frail elder — cause not yet determined
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sudden loss of mobility is an acute medical event requiring in-person assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute stroke
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Could reflect a neurological event; needs in-person neuro examination and imaging to exclude.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe dehydration with acute kidney injury
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced conscious level accompanies severe hypovolaemia and metabolic derangement.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Delirium from occult infection / sepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoactive delirium presents as drowsiness and withdrawal.

symptoms.symptom_name: Six-month unintentional significant weight loss, refractory to supplements

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe adult failure to thrive / terminal frailty
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive refractory weight loss is the core of the frailty/failure-to-thrive trajectory.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult malignancy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Significant unintentional weight loss is a cardinal red flag for malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry mouth and lips / reduced urine output

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe dehydration with acute kidney injury
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Carer-observable hydration proxy; the formal turgor/mucous-membrane assessment is sealed and clinician-only.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adverse effect of preventative polypharmacy (statin, strict antihypertensives)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inappropriate preventative medicines can drive anorexia and hypotension in end-stage frailty.

symptoms.symptom_name: Depressed mood and social withdrawal / apathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe major depression
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low mood, apathy and anorexia overlap with depression, a potentially treatable contributor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe adult failure to thrive / terminal frailty
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Apathy and withdrawal are common in the failure-to-thrive syndrome.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe dehydration with acute kidney injury
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician measurement; the AI cannot obtain a BP and must infer circulatory concern from reduced intake and obtundation, not a measured value.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adverse effect of preventative polypharmacy (statin, strict antihypertensives)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Strict antihypertensives compound hypotension in a dehydrated frail elder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor skin turgor and bone-dry oral mucosa

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe dehydration with acute kidney injury
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective hydration signs — clinician-only; sealed. The carer's 'dry lips' report is the admissible telehealth proxy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound cachexia, temporal wasting and global sarcopenia

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe adult failure to thrive / terminal frailty
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective cachexia supports advanced frailty; gauntness is partly video-observable but formal wasting assessment is clinician-only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stage II pressure injury, right greater trochanter

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute-on-chronic decompensation of a frail elder — cause not yet determined
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marks dangerous new immobility; requires in-person inspection and staging, not possible via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anticipated severe hypoalbuminaemia and low cholesterol

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe adult failure to thrive / terminal frailty
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Part of the malnutrition tetrad; bloods are hospital/GP investigations, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, tachycardia or focal neurological signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Delirium from occult infection / sepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring but non-exclusory — frail elders are frequently afebrile and non-localising in sepsis; these negatives are clinician-observed and do not clear the emergency remotely.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute-on-chronic decompensation requiring emergency in-person assessment
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, new confusion + 48h intake refusal + loss of weight-bearing + obtundation constitute a frail-elder emergency-until-excluded, mandating urgent in-person assessment regardless of the chronic backdrop.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent bloods — U&E, creatinine/eGFR, FBE, CRP, albumin, glucose, troponin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify dehydration/AKI, screen for infection/sepsis and a silent cardiac event, and document malnutrition. Obtained at in-person assessment — the AI arranges the assessment, it does not order or interpret bloods remotely.
Occult urinary infection is a common, treatable precipitant of delirium in frail elders.
Screen for a silent myocardial infarction/arrhythmia presenting only as collapse.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination (cardiorespiratory, abdominal, hydration, cognition/delirium screen, neuro, pressure-area survey)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The examination that separates a reversible precipitant from terminal decline and that the telehealth AI cannot perform.
